what_it_does: Records and communicates an architectural principle that the user interface must not be treated as a special exception to the company's typed, compositional system laws.
observations: YAML frontmatter follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type=block, source=claude_code, state=current, title prefixed 'A1 · Principle', channel=dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-b1f59e67.
History shows the item was filed and later reframed to 'background/problems/principles, no plan'.
Body is a short markdown principle statement.
It labels the interface as the one hand-coded monolithic exception to the company's laws and calls that exception 'the drift'.
It explicitly refuses to prescribe implementation, leaving how the interface joins the laws to 'Tim's architecture'.
standouts: Declares 'the interface is not a special case' as a governing principle.
Identifies the interface as the sole exception to the company's typed, compositional laws.
Explicitly defers all implementation resolution to 'Tim's architecture'.
Reframed from a plan into a principle statement.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema including id, type, links, timestamps, and history entries.
Uses a markdown heading and concise principle body.
Prefixes the title with 'A1 ·' to signal a foundational principle.
concerns: The principle is abstract and provides no concrete examples or acceptance criteria.
It relies on an external actor ('Tim's architecture') whose identity and scope are not defined in the file.
The boundary between principle and implementation plan is asserted but not demonstrated.
notes: A noticeboard principle stating the interface must obey the same architectural laws as the rest of the system, while deliberately leaving implementation to Tim's architecture.
questions: Who or what is 'Tim's architecture' in this context?
What are the specific typed, compositional laws the rest of the company follows?
What is the content or role of the parent item board://item-b1f59e67?
What concrete behavior or structural changes would satisfy this principle?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (a principle) matches the content; it reframes away from a plan and avoids prescribing implementation.
intention_signals: Establish a governing norm that removes the interface's privileged status and forces it into the same typed, compositional fabric as other company components, while deliberately avoiding premature implementation prescription.
novelty: Frames the interface exception as 'the drift' and explicitly refuses to design the solution, delegating resolution to a named architect.
contribution: Contributes a clear, reusable principle that the interface is a composition like any other, useful for evaluating architecture decisions without prescribing them.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item records the architectural principle that the user interface must obey the same typed, compositional laws as the rest of the company rather than remaining a hand-coded monolithic exception.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a governing norm that eliminates the interface's special-case status and aligns it with the company's existing architectural laws, leaving implementation to Tim's architecture.
